Will assigns a Spanish-themed week to New Directions and David helps by singing 'Sexy and I Know It' partially in Spanish.
The Australian ringneck is a parrot native to Australia.
Currently, four subspecies are recognised, each with a distinct range.
The basic colour is green, and all four subspecies have the characteristic yellow ring around the hindneck.
The calls of the mallee ringneck and Cloncurry parrot have been described as 'ringing'.
'Fifteen' is a country pop song performed by American singer-songwriter Taylor Swift.
The song was inspired by Swift's freshman year of high school at Hendersonville High School.
The song has Swift reminiscing on events that occurred to her and her best friend at the age of 15.
Swift wrote cautionary lyrics, intended to target teenage girls.
M-11 is a state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan in the Grand Rapids metropolitan area.
The highway runs through both rural woodlands and busy commercial areas before it terminates at another interchange with I-96 in Cascade Township.
The western terminus is at exit 24 on I-96, one mile east of Marne.
M-11 turns south on Wilson Avenue to run north-south through Walker.
The Sunol Water Temple is located at 505 Paloma Way in Sunol, California.
Designed by Willis Polk, the 59 foot high classical pavilion is made up of twelve concrete Corinthian columns.
Since the mid-19th century a private company, the Spring Valley Water Company (SVWC), owned much of the Alameda Creek Watershed.
Prior to the construction of the Hetch Hetchy Aqueduct, half of San Francisco's water supply passed through the Sunol temple.
'The Client' is the seventh episode of the second season of the American comedy television series The Office.
Michael Scott and Jan Levenson meet with a potential client, Christian, at Chili's.
The kiss between the two was rehearsed and filmed 'many, many, many times'.
The episode received mostly positive reviews from critics and earned a Nielsen rating of 3.8.
The Battle of Spencer's Ordinary was an inconclusive skirmish that took place on 26 June 1781.
British forces under Lieutenant Colonel John Graves Simcoe and American forces under Colonel Richard Butler clashed near a tavern.
Lafayette had been shadowing Cornwallis as he moved his army toward Williamsburg from central Virginia.
Both sides, concerned that the other might be reinforced by its main army, eventually broke off the battle.
Hurricane Able was the first named tropical cyclone in the Atlantic hurricane database.
It reached peak winds of 125 miles per hour, equivalent to a modern-day Category 3 hurricane.
The hurricane killed 2 people in Canada and caused over $1 million in damage.
In North Carolina, winds and waves brushed the coast, while around New York City, heavy rainfall caused some flooding.
The weapon dance employs weapons — or stylized versions of weapons — traditionally used in combat.
Such dancing is quite common to folk ritual in many parts of the world.
Many of the activities that humans have engaged in for millennia have traditionally found expression in various kinds of dance.
There is a wide range of such weapon dances in the world.
SMS Nymphe was the third member of the ten-ship Gazelle class, built by the Imperial German Navy.
She was commissioned into the High Seas Fleet in September 1900.
The ship had a long, if uneventful, career that spanned over thirty years.
She was armed with a main battery of ten 10.5 cm guns and two 45 cm torpedo tubes.
'Earth Angel' is a song by American doo-wop group the Penguins.
The song's origins lie in multiple different sources, among them songs by Jesse Belvin, Patti Page, and the Hollywood Flames.
The song became the first independent label release to appear on Billboard's national pop charts.
In 2005, it was one of 50 recordings chosen by the Library of Congress to be added to the National Recording Registry.
Resident Evil 4 is a survival horror third-person shooter video game developed and published by Capcom.
The story of Resident Evil 4 follows the U.S. government special agent Leon S. Kennedy.
Resident Evil 4 underwent a long development time during which four proposed versions of the game were discarded.
The game was intended to be a GameCube exclusive as part of the Capcom Five.
'Nothing Important Happened Today' is the collective name for the ninth season premiere of The X-Files.
The episode helped to explore the series' overarching mythology.
The episode introduced both the story arc about baby William which would continue to develop throughout the ninth season.
In Part 2, Scully, Reyes, and Doggett are led to a ship, where they find evidence of experimentation on human embryos.
Galveston is a coastal city located on Galveston Island and Pelican Island in the U.S. state of Texas.
The city served as the main port for the Texas Navy during the Texas Revolution.
During the 19th century, Galveston became a major U.S. commercial center and one of the largest ports in the United States.
Much of Galveston's modern economy is centered in the tourism, health care, shipping, and financial industries.
Banglapedia, or the National Encyclopedia of Bangladesh, is the first Bangladeshi encyclopedia.
The print version comprises ten 500-page volumes.
The Banglapedia project originated when the Asiatic Society of Bangladesh was working on a three-volume study titled History of Bangladesh.
The project officially took off in 1998.
FBI Assistant Director Walter Sergei Skinner is a fictional character in The X-Files.
Skinner supervised the X-Files office, which is concerned with cases with particularly mysterious or possibly supernatural circumstances.
Skinner is a former Marine and a Vietnam War veteran.
Initially, Skinner acts solely as a supervisor to Agent Mulder and Dana Scully's investigations of the paranormal.
'Leave It to Beaver' is the twenty-second and final episode of the first season of the American television series Veronica Mars.
The season finale concludes the storyline of Lilly Kane's murder.
Veronica discovers new evidence which suggests that Abel Koontz is innocent.
Things get more complicated when Veronica falls into a relationship with Lilly's ex-boyfriend Logan Echolls.

He later served as the Governor for Trinidad and Tobago.
Carter joined the Royal Navy in 1864, serving as an Assistant Clerk on the HMS Frederick William.
Blackout represents a musical departure from Spears' earlier work.
The lyrical themes revolve around fame, media scrutiny, sex, and clubbing.
Upon its release, Blackout received mixed to positive reviews from music critics.
The Times named it as the fifth best pop album of the decade.
The Hillsboro Civic Center is a government-built, mixed-use development in downtown Hillsboro, Oregon.
The complex was built to centralize city government functions under one roof.
The building was awarded the LEED Gold certification for sustainability.
Environmentally friendly technologies used include occupancy sensors and solar panels.
State Route 7 (SR 7) is a state highway in Lewis and Pierce counties, located in the U.S. state of Washington.
The road has several names, such as Second Street in Morton and the Mountain Highway in rural areas.
The earliest road to use the current route of SR 7 first appeared in a 1900 map of the Tacoma area.
During the 1964 highway renumbering, the branches became SR 7.
The Rebel Flesh is the fifth episode of the sixth series of the British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
The episode features the TARDIS being hit by a solar storm.
The Doctor, Amy, and Rory must work to prevent the two groups from breaking into a war.
Workers have utilized a 'programmable matter' called the Flesh.
Mycena galericulata is a mushroom species commonly known as the common bonnet.
The caps can reach 4 cm in diameter, and have a mealy odor and taste.
The mushrooms have caps with distinct radial grooves, particularly at the margin.
The species can generally be considered inedible.
SMS Prinzess Wilhelm was a protected cruiser of the German Imperial Navy.
She was deployed to East Asian waters, where she frequently served as the flagship of the East Asia Cruiser Division.
She was armed with a main battery of fourteen 15 cm guns and had a top speed of 18 knots.
The ship was reduced to a mine hulk in February 1914.
The 12th Light Horse Regiment was a mounted unit of the Australian Army.
It was raised in New South Wales in 1915 as part of the Australian Imperial Force.
The regiment served in the Sinai and Palestine campaign against the Ottoman Empire.
It was disbanded in late 1919.
Reaganing is the fifth episode of the fifth season of the American television comedy series 30 Rock.
Jack Donaghy is succeeding at all of his tasks.
The episode was watched by 5.182 million households during its original broadcast.
Matt Hubbard received a Primetime Emmy Award nomination for his work in this episode.
A reflector sight or reflex sight is an optical device that allows the user to look through a partially reflecting glass element.
These sights work on the simple optical principle that anything at the focus of a lens or curved mirror will look like it is sitting in front of the viewer at infinity.
Since their invention in 1900, reflector sights have come to be used as gun sights on all kinds of weapons.
They were used on fighter aircraft, in a limited capacity in World War I, widely used in World War II.
Silent Hill 2 is a survival horror video game published by Konami for the PlayStation 2.
The game centers on James Sunderland, who enters the town after receiving a letter written by his deceased wife.
Silent Hill 2 received critical acclaim from critics.
It is now considered by many critics to be the greatest horror game of all time.
The X-Files Mythology – Volume 3 collection is the third DVD release containing selected episodes from the fifth to the eighth seasons.
The episodes follow the investigations of paranormal-related cases by FBI Special Agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully.
The episodes are centered on alien colonization efforts and the fall of the Syndicate.
Events covered include the discovery of alien-human hybrid Cassandra Spender.
Thalaba the Destroyer is an 1801 epic poem composed by Robert Southey.
The story describes how a group of sorcerers work to destroy the Hodeirah family.
Southey uses the poem to describe various superstitions and myths.
Although based in Islamic theology, most of the action is mechanical instead of emphasizing possible moral truths.
Hurricane Richard was a damaging hurricane that affected areas of Central America in October 2010.
The storm intensified into hurricane status, reaching maximum winds of 100 mph.
Hurricane Richard caused an estimated $80 million in damages in its path.
In Belize, most of the damage was attributed to damage to crops.
Ralph Wild Larkin is an American sociologist and research consultant.
He is the author of Suburban Youth in Cultural Crisis.
Larkin met fellow sociologist Daniel A. Foss while teaching at Rutgers University.
They have frequently partnered in research on the study of social movements.
Devadasu is a 1953 Indian bilingual romance film directed by Vedantam Raghavaiah.
The film focuses on Devadasu and Parvati, who have been in love since childhood.
Devadasu is the inaugural recipient of the Filmfare Award for Best Film – Telugu.
Both versions were critically and commercially successful.
The BR Standard Class 7, otherwise known as the Britannia Class, is a class of 4-6-2 Pacific steam locomotive.
Fifty-five were constructed between 1951 and 1954.
The Britannia Class was based on several previous LMS locomotive designs.
The design was a result of the 1948 locomotive exchanges.
My Gym Partner's a Monkey is an American animated television series that premiered on Cartoon Network.
The story revolves around Adam Lyon, a human who is forced to transfer to a school for local anthropomorphic zoo animals.
The series aired 96 episodes, a special, and a feature film during its run.
It was met with generally positive reviews from critics.
Great Northern Highway links Western Australia's capital city Perth with its northernmost port, Wyndham.
It is the longest highway in Australia, with a length of almost 3,200 kilometres.
Economically, it provides vital access through the Wheatbelt and Mid West to the resource-rich regions of the Pilbara and Kimberley.
The highway was created in 1944 from existing roads in the Wheatbelt.
The music for the Drakengard series has been handled by multiple composers since the release of the original game in 2004.
Drakengard's composers were Nobuyoshi Sano and Takayuki Aihara.
The soundtracks have received mixed to positive reviews from both dedicated video game music outlets and video game critics.
The first game was noted for its disturbing style.

Both Mogador and her sister Volta were present during the British attack on Mers-el-Kébir.

The album is made up of Christmas-themed songs, and was recorded in the middle of 2008.
Lieutenant General Sir Humfrey Myddelton Gale was an officer in the British Army who served in the First and Second World War.
After the Second World War he was European Director of the United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration.
Maryland Route 355 is a 36.75-mile north-south road in western central Maryland.

It is one of the most-spoken Berber languages, along with Kabyle, Shilha, Riff, and Shawiya.
Tamazight has a phonemic three-vowel system, but also has numerous words without vowels.
Pervasive borrowing from Arabic extends to all major word classes, including verbs.

Two music videos for the single were released.
The first video features Kesha and her friends on a distant planet, dancing around in a mosh pit.
Z15 Erich Steinbrinck was a Type 1934A-class destroyer built for Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
The ship was named after the First World War German naval officer Erich Steinbrinck.
At the beginning of World War II on 1 September 1939, the ship was initially deployed to blockade the Polish coast.

Sandsfoot saw service during the English Civil War.

Sheng nu is a derogatory term made popular by the All-China Women's Federation.

Wind shear is a difference in wind speed and/or direction over a relatively short distance in the atmosphere.
It is commonly observed near microbursts and downbursts caused by thunderstorms.
Wind shear has a significant effect during take-off and landing of aircraft.
It has been a sole or contributing cause of many aircraft accidents.

The 2009 Atlantic hurricane season was a below-average Atlantic hurricane season that produced eleven tropical cyclones
The season 's first tropical cyclone , Tropical Depression One , developed on May 28
The most intense hurricane , Bill , was a powerful Cape Verde-type hurricane that affected areas from the Leeward Islands to Newfoundland
Tropical Storm Ana brought substantial rainfall totals to many of the Caribbean islands

In this episode , Dunder Mifflin prepares for the launch of their new website
Dwight tries to outsell the website , Andy makes a move for Angela , and Michael kidnaps a pizza delivery boy
Michael returns to Scranton dejected and attempts to plan a better party

Charles Robert Forbes ( February 14 , 1878 – April 10 , 1952 ) was appointed the first Director of the Veterans ' Bureau by President Warren G. Harding
His tenure as the first Veterans ' Bureau director was characterized by corruption and scandal
Forbes was considered the " dashing playboy " of Washington and a favorite of President Harding
After the 1920 U.S. Presidential election , President Harding appointed Forbes director to the newly created Veterans ' Bureau
King of Pop is a compilation album by American recording artist Michael Jackson released in commemoration of Michael Jackson 's 50th birthday
The album 's title comes from the title Jackson acquired approximately 20 years earlier
Fans in countries where Sony BMG operates national offices were given the opportunity to vote for songs from a list of Jackson 's back catalogue

He was blinded along with his rebellious father Álmos on the order of Álmos 's brother , King Coloman of Hungary
Two violent purges were carried out among the partisans of his predecessors to strengthen Béla 's rule
In the second half of Béla 's reign , Hungary adopted an active foreign policy
The Petlyakov Pe-8 was a Soviet heavy bomber designed before World War II
Produced in limited numbers , it was used to bomb Berlin in August 1941
Its primary mission , however , was to attack German airfields , rail yards and other rear-area facilities at night

evidence: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights.
To derogate from the representation may amount to an abuse of power or unfairness.
The doctrine of legitimate expectation as a ground to quash decisions of public authorities has been firmly established by the English courts.
Tea & Sympathy is the debut solo album by Australian musician Bernard Fanning.
The album blends alternative and country-folk music.
Most of the record was written after the cancer related death of his brother in 2002.
His brother's death coincided with the end of Fanning's twelve-year relationship with his partner.
Telengard is a 1982 role-playing dungeon crawler video game developed by Daniel Lawrence.
The player explores a dungeon, fights monsters with magic, and avoids traps in real time.
Lawrence first wrote the game as DND, a 1976 version of Dungeons & Dragons for the DECsystem-10 mainframe computer.
Honey is a song by American electronica musician Moby.
The uptempo song incorporates vocal samples from Sometimes by American blues singer Bessie Jones.
Moby discovered the Sometimes sample while listening to albums of folk music recordings compiled by field collector Alan Lomax.
James Graham was an Irish non-commissioned officer in the British Army during the Napoleonic wars.
He was commended for his gallantry during the defence of Hougoumont, at Waterloo.
Graham saved the life of an officer, and his own brother, and was among the small group responsible for closing the North Gate at Hougoumont.
Meralda Elva Junior Warren is an artist and poet of Pitcairn Island.
Her book, Mi Base side orn Pitcairn, written with the island's six children, is the first to be written and published in both English and Pitkern.
Warren has also published a cookbook featuring Pitcairn Island cuisine.
She has also served as the island's nurse, its only police officer, and a member of the territory's governing council.
The 2001 American Memorial was a Championship Auto Racing Teams (CART) motor race held on September 15, 2001.
Kenny Bräck won the race for Team Rahal.
The race's name was changed by CART in the aftermath of the September 11 attacks.
The crash led to the amputation of both of Zanardi's legs.
The Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-3 was a Soviet interceptor and fighter aircraft used during World War II.
It replaced the MiG-1 on the production line at Factory No. 1 on 20 December 1940.
The large number of defects noted during flight testing of the MiG-1 forced Mikoyan and Gurevich to make a number of modifications to the design.
SMS Wacht was an aviso of the Imperial German Navy, the lead ship of her class.
Her career was cut short on 4 September 1901, when she collided with the old ironclad SMS Sachsen.
Wacht was built by the AG Weser shipyard; she was laid down in 1886, launched in August 1887, and commissioned in August 1888.
Three Words is the sixteenth episode of the eighth season of The X-Files.
The episode explores the series' overarching mythology.
Mulder secretly conducts his own investigation after a man is gunned down on the White House lawn attempting to inform the President of a planned alien invasion.
The Laguna Madre is a long, shallow, hypersaline lagoon along the western coast of the Gulf of Mexico.
It is separated by the roughly 20-mile long Saltillo Flats land bridge into Upper and Lower lagoons.
The lagoon's ecosystem is protected by the Laguna Atascosa National Wildlife Refuge and the Padre Island National Seashore.
The 138th Boat Race took place on 4 April 1992.
Oxford won by one-and-a-quarter lengths, the closest margin of victory for twenty years.
The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Nicholas Theodore 'Nick' Nemeth, best known by the ring name Dolph Ziggler, is an American professional wrestler.
He is signed to WWE on the SmackDown brand.
Nemeth signed a development contract with WWE in 2004, and was sent to Ohio Valley Wrestling (OVW).
Since then, Ziggler has held the World Heavyweight Championship twice.
The Hawker Hunter is a transonic British jet aircraft developed in the late 1940s and early 1950s.
The single-seat Hunter entered service as a manoeuvrable fighter aircraft.
Hunters were also used by two RAF display teams: the 'Black Arrows', who on one occasion looped a record-breaking 24 examples in formation.
The Doughnut is the nickname given to the headquarters of the Government Communications Headquarters (GCHQ).
It houses 5,500 employees; GCHQ is the largest single employer in Gloucestershire.
The construction of the Doughnut in 2003 consolidated the operations previously spread across two sites into a single location.
John Kourkouas was one of the most important generals of the Byzantine Empire.
His success in battles against the Muslim states in the East definitively reversed the course of the centuries-long Byzantine – Arab Wars.
As commander of an imperial bodyguard regiment, Kourkouas was among the chief supporters of Emperor Romanos I Lekapenos.
John Wilson Bengough was one of Canada's first cartoonists, as well as an editor, publisher, writer, poet, entertainer, and politician.
He published some cartoons under the pen name L. Côté.
Bengough is best remembered for his political cartoons in Grip, a satirical magazine he published and edited.
The Riverview Theater is a neighborhood movie theater in the Howe neighborhood of Minneapolis, Minnesota.
The theater was built by theater owners Bill and Sidney Volk in 1948.
The Riverview remains one of several surviving single-screen cinemas in the Minneapolis – Saint Paul area.

The opera was immediately successful from its first night on 21 November 1831 at the Opéra.
The Vinyl Conflict is a box set by the thrash metal band Slayer, released October 12, 2010.
It includes all ten of Slayer's studio albums since 1986 and their 1991 live album Decade of Aggression.
Sava Atanasov Mutkurov was a Bulgarian officer (Major General) and politician.
He was among the chief architects of the Bulgarian unification (1885).
Empire Endurance was a 8,514 GRT cargo liner that was built in 1928.
She was requisitioned by the Kriegsmarine for use as a supply ship.
Sjumandjaja was an Indonesian director, screenwriter, and actor.
He won five Citra Awards from the Indonesian Film Festival.
From the Ages is the third full-length studio album by American rock band Earthless.
The album comprises four songs, all instrumental, and was recorded in two days.
The Sulu bleeding-heart is a species of bird in the pigeon and dove family.
It is currently listed as critically endangered by the IUCN.
Guilty Gear X is a fighting game developed by Arc System Works.
It was released in July 2000 for Japanese arcades.
Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland, BWV 61, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
He composed it in Weimar for the first Sunday in Advent.
The F Word is the twelfth episode of the thirteenth season of South Park.
The episode argues language is ever-changing and that taboo words only carry a stigma if society allows them to.
The Siege of Inabayama Castle of 1567 was the final battle in Oda Nobunaga's campaign.
The siege ended in a decisive battle and victory of Nobunaga's combined forces.
A beefsteak is a type of banquet in which sliced beef tenderloin is served.
The dining style originated in 19th-century New York City as a type of working-class celebration.
The Price of Gold is the fourth episode of the American fairy tale/drama television series Once Upon a Time.
The episode centers on Emma Swan coming to the aid of a young pregnant woman.
Caernarfon Castle is a medieval fortress in Caernarfon, Gwynedd, north-west Wales.
The Edwardian town and castle acted as the administrative centre of north Wales.
The 2002 Gator Bowl was a post-season American college football bowl game.
The game ended in a 30-17 victory for Florida State.
Edward William Bastard was an English cricketer who played for Oxford University and Somerset.
He was described in his Wisden obituary as Somerset's best bowler during his time with the club.
SummerSlam (1993) was the sixth annual SummerSlam professional wrestling pay-per-view event.
It took place on August 30, 1993, at The Palace of Auburn Hills in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
Satan's Harvest Home is a pamphlet published anonymously in 1749 in London.
It describes and denounces what it deems the moral laxity and perversion of contemporary society.
The Asian house martin is a migratory passerine bird of the swallow family Hirundinidae.
This species is locally abundant and is expanding northward in Siberia.
The German destroyer Z2 Georg Thiele was a Type 1934 destroyer built for the Kriegsmarine.
She was initially deployed to blockade the Polish coast.

evidence: Wilfrid Eggleston OBE (25 March 1901 – 13 June 1986) was an Anglo-Canadian journalist, author and civil servant.
Joining the civil service through his productive membership of the secretariat in the Rowell-Sirois Commission of 1937.
Wilfrid Eggleston was born on 25 March 1901 in Lincoln to English parents who had moved from Spalding two years earlier.
NSB Class 64 (Norwegian: NSB type 64) is a class of three electric multiple units built by Strømmens Værksted for the Norwegian State Railways.
Delivered in 1935, they were built for the opening of the Hardanger Line and served there until 1985.
The Hardanger Line opened in 1935 as a steep and curvy branch of the Bergen Line to connect Bergen to the Hardangerfjord.
The Harvey brothers are six siblings from Victoria, Australia, known primarily for their success in the sport of cricket between the mid-1930s and the early 1960s.
Neil Harvey represented Australia in 79 Test matches between 1948 and 1963.
The sons of Horace and Elsie Harvey, in chronological order, they are Merv (1918 – 1995), Clarence (aka 'Mick'; b. 1921), Harold (b. 1923), Ray (b. 1926), Neil (b. 1928) and Brian (1932 – 1969).
Dareka no Manazashi (Japanese: だれかのまなざし, lit. Someone's Gaze) is a Japanese anime short film written and directed by Makoto Shinkai.
The story develops through reminiscent scenes of their family life and the connection the girl and her father share with the family's cat.
The film was described as 'deeply emotional', particularly for older viewers due to its emphasis on family bonds as a family changes over time.
'Call the Shots' is a song by British girl group Girls Aloud from their fourth studio album, Tangled Up (2007).
The song showcases a more mature side from Girls Aloud and received positive reviews from music critics.
In September 2007, 'Call the Shots' leaked online, and on 26 November of the same year, it was released as the second single from Tangled Up.
'1 + 1' is a song recorded by American recording artist Beyoncé for her fourth studio album, 4 (2011).
A down-tempo contemporary R & B and soul music power ballad, '1 + 1' Beyoncé expressing her endless love to her soul mate.
The accompanying music video was directed by Beyoncé herself, alongside Lauren Briet and Ed Burke, and it premiered on August 26, 2011.
'First Strike' is the 60th episode and the third season finale of the science fiction television series Stargate Atlantis.
The episode is the first of a three-parter, in which the new Earth ship Apollo arrives at Atlantis to conduct a preemptive strike against the Asurans.
The episode earned Stargate Atlantis the strongest ratings since the mid-season hiatus and was generally well received.
The women's skeleton event at the 2010 Winter Olympics took place at the Whistler Sliding Centre on 18 – 19 February.
The competition was won by British athlete Amy Williams, who set new course records for the track on her first and third runs.
Williams' victory was not without controversy, as the United States and Canada filed complaints with the judges related to Williams' helmet.
Bedford Castle was a large medieval castle in Bedford, England.
Built after 1100 by Henry I, the castle played a prominent part in both the civil war of the Anarchy and the First Barons' War.
Henry III of England besieged the castle in 1224 following a disagreement with Falkes de Breauté; the siege lasted eight weeks and involved an army of as many as 2,700 soldiers.
Live Intrusion is a home video by Slayer which was released in 1995 through American Recordings and filmed at the Mesa Amphitheater in Mesa, Arizona on March 12, 1995.
The video features a cover of the Venom song 'Witching Hour' performed by Slayer.
Live Intrusion received positive reception from the few critics that reviewed the video.
The First Motion Picture Unit (FMPU) was the primary film production unit of the US Army Air Forces during World War II.
It produced more than 400 propaganda and training films.
The success of Winning Your Wings created a demand for training and recruitment films which proved difficult for Warner Bros. to fulfill.
A pail closet or pail privy was a room used for the disposal of human excreta, under the pail system of waste removal.
The pail closet was an evolution of the midden closet.
The pail system was popular in France and England, particularly in the historic Lancashire town of Rochdale.
Occupy is a short study of the Occupy movement written by the American academic and political activist Noam Chomsky.
With the birth of the Occupy Movement – devoted to socio-political change – in 2011, Chomsky became a vocal supporter for the protesters.
Chomsky was a prominent critic of capitalism, the role of western media and the foreign policy of the U.S. government.
Delaware Route 30 (DE 30) is a state highway in Sussex County, Delaware.
The route runs from an intersection with Bi-State Boulevard a short distance west of U.S. Route 13.
What is now DE 30 south of Gravel Hill was built as a state highway in various stages during the 1920s and 1930s.
Chiswick Bridge is a reinforced concrete deck arch bridge over the River Thames in west London.
One of three bridges opened in 1933 as part of an ambitious scheme to relieve traffic congestion west of London.
The construction of the road required two new bridges to be built, at Twickenham and Chiswick.
Consciousness is the state or quality of awareness, or, of being aware of an external object or something within oneself.
It has been defined as: sentience, awareness, subjectivity, the ability to experience or to feel.
Western philosophers, since the time of Descartes and Locke, have struggled to comprehend the nature of consciousness and pin down its essential properties.
Peter de Maulay or Peter de Mauley (died 1241) was a nobleman and administrator who was one of King John of England's 'evil counsellors'.
During the rebellions of the end of John's reign, Maulay supported the king and was given custody of the king's younger son.
Maulay continued to serve the new king after 1216 but ran into difficulties with the regents for the young King Henry III.
The Entertainment Software Rating Board (ESRB) is a self-regulatory organization that assigns age and content ratings.
The ESRB was established in 1994 by the Entertainment Software Association.
Despite its positive reception, the ESRB has still faced criticism from politicians and other watchdog groups for the structure of its operations.
Tamar the Great reigned as Queen regnant of Georgia from 1184 to 1213, presiding over the apex of the Georgian Golden Age.
Tamar was successful in neutralizing this opposition and embarked on an energetic foreign policy.
Tamar's association with the period of political and military successes and cultural achievements has led to her idealization and romanticization in Georgian arts and historical memory.
Air Commodore Sir Hughie Idwal Edwards, VC, KCMG, CB, DSO, OBE, DFC was a senior officer in the Royal Air Force.
Edwards was decorated with the Victoria Cross in 1941 for his efforts in leading a bombing raid against the port of Bremen.
Serving throughout the Second World War, he gained a permanent commission and continued his career in the RAF after the war.
